<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Mr Oman Herbert-Blankson
14 Lanchester Way
New Cross
London
SE14 5HQ
The Disclosures Team,
Everything Everywhere,
Hatfield Business Park,
Hertfordshire,
AL10 9BW

28 July 2013

Dear Sir/Madam,

Reference No: 786113Ο²¬† No. 07817 234 646

Re: A Subject Access Request for Debt (Mr Oman Herbert-Blankson
14 Lanchester Way, New Cross, London, SE14 5HQ)

I am writing to make a subject access request under the Data Protection Act 1998 for any personal information you hold about me.

Please supply me with all data that you hold on me. This includes in particular, but is not limited to, the following:-

1. The original signed, executed credit agreement and any terms and conditions that applied to the account at the time the account was opened.

2. Where there has been any event in my account history over this period which has required manual intervention by any person, I require disclosure of any indication or notes which have either caused or resulted in that manual intervention, or other evidence of that manual intervention in relation to my account held with orange.

3. Documents relating to any insurance added to the account, including the insurance contract and terms and conditions, date it was added and deleted.

5. Details of any collection charges added to the account; specifically, the date it was levied, the amount of the charge, a detailed financial Breakdown of how the charges were calculated, and what the charge charges cover.

6. Specific details of the fees/charges levied by any other agency in respect of this account/these accounts and a detailed breakdown of said fees/charges and what each charge relates to and on what date said fees/charges were levied.

7. A genuine copy of any notice of fair use of my data as required by the Data Protection Act 1998

8. A list of third party agencies to whom you have disclosed my personal data and a summary of the nature of the information you have disclosed.

9. Copies of statements for the entire duration of the credit agreement.

I enclose the statutory maximum fee of Ο²¬Θ10. You have 40 days in which to comply. If you have any queries or questions then please contact me on the above address.

Yours Faithfully,

Mr Oman Herbert-Blankson

Dear Sir,

I am writing this letter to introduce my company to organisation which has been in business for the past 4 years. Please find enclosed my certificate of registration.

We have been an established and popular company with an excellent track record for good customer satisfaction. We have never compromised on the quality and the services provided to the customer. We believe in keeping the customers happy and providing them with products at a very competent price.

Please feel free to keep in touch with us about any of your business related requirements/queries. As I wouldΟ²¬†like to offer our service to your company.

We look forward to a wonderful relationship together and success for all concerned.

Kind regards

Oman Herbert-Blankson

Transparent, good communication and a friendly knowledgable point of contact

Best wishes

ATTENTION ALL LANDLORDS

Divas Property Consultants are a leading Londonwide property letting and RENT GUARANTEE Specialists. We have waiting professional tenants and a high success rate of finding YOU the right tenant.

We charge 2 weeks rent LET ONLY service and Full Management 5% no set up costs!

*No renewal fees or on-going charges

We offer a highly professional and efficient range of services and believe in a close and honest relationship between both landlord and agent in addition to tenant and agent.

As specialists we keep up to date on all laws and regulations within the lettings market. We also understand that in todayΟΔβ²§β³Δs world you are very busy and need an efficient and professional service with excellence as a minimum standard!

We are Open 7 days a week, 9am to 8pm Monday to Friday and 10am to 7pm Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

### Source: paapa_s notebook/MACADAMIA Fruit Facts.md

title: MACADAMIA Fruit Facts.md
updated: 2026-03-10T16:04:58.0000000Z

---
title: MACADAMIA Fruit Facts
updated: 2013-03-24 08:42:16Z
created: 2013-03-24 08:42:16Z
source: http://www.crfg.org/pubs/ff/macadamia.html
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

# MACADAMIA

<img src="../_resources/3b069063fac8dafbb4b11a3e95f265ac.gif" width="228" height="243" alt="B/W sketch"/>

## *Macadamia* spp.

## *Proteaceae*

**Common Names:**Macadamia, Australian nut, Queensland Nut.

**Species:** "Smooth-shelled Macadamia" (*Macadamia integrifolia* Maiden & Betche), "Rough-shelled Macadamia" (*M. tetraphylla* L. Johnson). Hybrid forms exist between the two species.

**Distant Affinity:** Helicia nut (*Athertonia diversifolia*), Chilean Hazel (*Gevuina avellana*), Australian Rosenut (*Hicksbeachia pinnatifolia*).

**Origin:**  *Macadamia integrifolia* is native to southeastern Queensland where it grows in the rain forests and close to streams. *M. tetraphylla* is native to southeastern Queensland and northeastern New South Wales, growing in rain forests, in moist places and along stream banks. At the point where the two species meet, there are types that appear to be natural hybrids. The macadamia was introduced into Hawaii about 1881 where it was used as an ornamental and for reforestation. The Hawaii Agricultural Experiment Station named and introduced several promising selections in 1948, which led to the modern macadamia industry in Hawaii. In California two seedling macadamias were planted in the early 1880's and are still standing on the Berkeley campus of the University of California. The importation of improved and named varieties into California from Hawaii began about 1950. Macadamias are also commercially important in Australia, South Africa and Central America.

**Adaptation:** Macadamias are ideally suited to a mild, frost-free climate with abundant rainfall distributed throughout the year, roughly the same climate suitable for growing coffee. Both species, however, grow well in the coastal areas of California, although varieties often respond differently to a given location. Mature macadamia trees are fairly frost hardy, tolerating temperatures as low as 24Ο²¬ΑΟ²¬†F, but the flower clusters are usually killed at 28Ο²¬ΑΟ²¬†F. Young trees can be killed by light frosts. *M. tetraphylla* appears to be slightly more cold-tolerant. Consistently high summer temperatures will reduce yields, although again *M. tetraphylla* shows more tolerance. When grown in a large tub, macadamias make suitable container plants.

## DESCRIPTION

**Growth Habit:** Macadamias are large, spreading evergreen trees reaching 30 to 40Ο²¬†ft. high and almost as wide. More upright types are known and being selected because of their suitability for closer planting. The bark is rough but unfurrowed, brown and dark red when cut. The macadamia has proteoid roots, dense clusters of short lateral rootlets in well defined rows around the parent root axis. The prime function of such roots appears to be in increasing the surface area of the root system for maximum absorption. The vigor of seedlings appears to be related to the degree of proteoid root development.

**Foliage:** The two species are fairly easily distinguished by their foliage. The leaves of *M. integrifolia* are 8 to 11Ο²¬†inches in length and occur usually in whorls of 3. The adult leaves are entire with few spines. New growth is pale green. The spiny, often sessile leaves of *M. tetraphylla* usually appear in whorls of 4 and may grow to 20Ο²¬†inches long. The new growth is bronzy pink. Growth in mature trees of both species occurs in two flushes, in spring and midsummer. In young trees four flushes may occur.

**Flowers:** Flowers are borne on long narrow racemes arising from the axils of leaves or the scars of fallen leaves. They may be borne on the new growth if it is mature, but more often on the two, or three season's growth preceding the most recently matured flushes. The flowers, about 1/2Ο²¬†inch long, are perfect but incomplete in that they have no petals, but four petaloid sepals.*M. integrifolia* has creamy white flowers borne in clusters 6 to 12Ο²¬†inches long, while the flowers of *M. tetraphylla* are cream-colored or pink and borne in clusters up to 15Ο²¬†inches long. Macadamias can self-pollinate, although varieties vary from being totally self-compatible to being almost self-sterile. Wind pollination may play some role, but bees are apparently the major agent in pollination. Cross-pollination by hand has been shown to increase nut set and quality.

**Fruit:** Macadamia nuts have a very hard seed coat enclosed in a green husk that splits open as the nut matures. As the common name indicates, this seed coat is smooth in the case of *M. integrifolia.* It holds a creamy white kernel containing up to 80%Ο²¬†oil and 4%Ο²¬†sugar. When roasted it develops a uniform color and texture. Although *M. tetraphylla* is often referred to as the rough-shelled macadamia, the seed coat of some cultivars are smooth, while others are rough and pebbled. The quality of the kernels of *M. tetraphylla*are also more variable. The oil content ranges from 65% to 75% and sugar content ranges from 6% to 8%. These factors result in variable color and texture when the the nuts are roasted under the same conditions as those of*M. integrifolia.*  *M. tetraphylla* is well suited to the home garden, however, and has been planted for commercial production in California.

## CULTURE

**Location:** Macadamias do best in full sun, although in hot climates partial shade can be beneficial. Windy locations should also be avoided. The brittle branches can be damaged by wind, especially when laden with a heavy crop of nuts.

**Soil:** Macadamias will perform on a wide range of soil types from open sands and lava rock soils to heavy clay soils, as long as the soil is well drained. They do best, however, in deep, rich soils with a pH of 5.5 to 6.5. Macadamias will not tolerate soil or water with high salt concentrations. In areas with low annual rainfall, leach the soil regularly.

**Irrigation:** Macadamias can withstand periods of drought, but the harvests will be small and of low quality. Irrigation seems to be more important during certain critical periods in the crop cycle, particularly from the time of nut set, through nut filling and through the vegetative growth period in midsummer. The trees should receive at least as much water as is normally provided an avocado tree. The actual amount depends on the soil. Young trees also have higher water requirements than mature trees. In general it is important to water macadamias regularly and deeply during dry periods.

**Fertilization:** Since macadamias grow slowly, they do not require large quantities of nitrogen fertilizer. Six months after planting out the trees should receive light applications of a balanced fertilizer such as a citrus mix or fish emulsion which contains no more than 1%Ο²¬†nitrogen. Applications should be made at least twice a year. A mature tree should receive approximately 5Ο²¬†pounds of citrus mix per application and young trees proportionally less. Too much nitrogen may result in chlorosis. Micronutrient deficiencies are common in some areas, but these can be corrected with chelated sprays.

**Pruning:** The object of pruning a macadamia is to form a tree with a single main stem and a framework of horizontal branches, starting at 3Ο²¬†ft. above the ground and from there at intervals of about 1-1/2Ο²¬†ft. In *M. integrifolia*there are 3 buds in a vertical row in each of the three leaf axils of a node. When the stem is is topped, all three upper buds will grow straight up. Only one of them must be allowed to remain and to continue the main stem, the other two being clipped off to a stub of about 3/8Ο²¬†inch. Now the buds below those two stubs will grow out in a more or less horizontal direction. Only these branches will flower and fruit. This process is repeated until a good framework has been established. Macadamias will take heavy pruning but this may drastically reduces yields.

**Frost Protection:** Frost protection is more critical for young trees than more mature ones. While they are still on the small side, the plants can be given the standard methods of protection, such as plastic sheeting and such draped over a frame around the tree. As the trees get larger, they are more difficult to cover, but they also become more tolerant of mild frosts

**Propagation:** Macadamias are easily grown from seed, but the seedlings may take 8 to 12Ο²¬†years to bear a crop and the quality of the nuts is unpredictable. Grafting is the most common method of producing nursery trees and is best done in spring or autumn. The wood of macadamia is hard, however, requiring the propagator to have experience to be successful. The scionwood is girdled some 6 to 8Ο²¬†weeks beforehand, the preferred wood being healthy mature material of the previous flush. The recommended graft is the simple whip, using material 3/8 to 5/8Ο²¬†inch thick. The side graft is also successful, and tip, wedge or cleft grafting is used under greenhouse conditions for working small seedlings up to 1Ο²¬†ft. high. Budding is also possible as well as propagation from softwood cutting and air-layering. Cutting-grown trees take some time to develop an adequate root system and will need staking when young. Some grafted varieties of macadamias begin bearing within 2Ο²¬†years, while others not for 7 to 8Ο²¬†years.

**Pests and Diseases:** In Australia there are a host of pests and diseases that afflict macadamias, but in the U.S. there are few problems in home gardens. Occasionally, thrips, mites and scale may be troublesome, and anthracnose can infect leaves and nuts in humid climates. Canker can also result from wounds to the tree. Macadamias are fairly resistant to *Phytophthora cinnamoni*, and are sometimes used to replant avocado orchards infected with the fungus. The roots of the macadamia do not appear to be very attractive to gophers, but deer will browse on the new foliage.

**Harvest:** Mature macadamia nuts will fall to the ground from late fall to spring. It is best to harvest fallen nuts, since shaking the trees to dislodge the nuts may also bring down immature nuts. A long pole can be used to carefully knock down mature nuts that are out of reach. A reasonably good tree will produce 30-50Ο²¬†pounds of nuts at 10Ο²¬†years age and gradually increase for many years.

Harvested nuts should be dehusked and spread in a dry place protected from the sun and allowed to dry for 2 or 3Ο²¬†weeks. To finish drying put the nuts in a shallow pan and place in the oven at the lowest temperature setting (100Ο²¬Α to 115Ο²¬ΑΟ²¬†F) for about 12Ο²¬†hours. Stir occasionally and watch that the nuts do not cook. Excessive heating will damage nut quality. Store the nuts in a cool, dry area. A heavy plastic bag will prevent nuts from reabsorbing moisture. When the nuts are dry, the shells can be removed with a nutcracker. A cottage industry of sorts has developed around designing nutcrackers that can best cope with the hard shells.

To home-roast macadamia nuts, place shelled nuts (whole kernels or halves only) in a shallow pan no more than two deep. Roast 40 to 50Ο²¬†minutes, stirring occasionally. Watch carefully and remove from the oven as soon as they start to turn tan. After roasting, the nuts store nicely, salted or unsalted, in airtight jars at 40Ο²¬Α to 65Ο²¬ΑΟ²¬†F. They can also be frozen. Macadamia nuts are excellent raw or roasted. In addition to being a quality snack, they can be used in almost any recipe that calls for nuts, including stuffings, fruit salads, cakes, etc.

**Commercial Potential:** Macadamia nuts are considered by many to be the prime edible nut. Even at the high prices demanded, twice that of cashews, the market remains unfilled. This demand for macadamia nuts has spurred a flurry of plantings in areas all over the world where macadamias will thrive. There is a limited but significant commercial production of the nuts in Southern California.

## CULTIVARS

**Beaumont** (Dr. Beaumont)

Hybrid. Originated in Australia. Discovered by Dr. J. H. Beaumont. Introduced in 1965 by the California Macadamia Society. Round, medium to large nut, 65 to 80Ο²¬†per pound. Shell medium-thick, kernel 40% of nut, with a high percentage of grade A kernels. Some nuts may split on the tree and be ruined. Texture and flavor very good. Tree upright, ornamental. New leaves reddish, flowers bright pink, borne on long racemes. Nuts drop over a long period. Recommended for home gardens.

**Burdick**

*M. tetraphylla.* Originated in Encinitas, Calif. Large nut, averaging 40Ο²¬†per pound. Shell thin, about 1/16Ο²¬†inch thick, well-filled. Kernel averages about 34% of total nut weight, quality good. Matures in October. Tree bears annually. Not widely planted these days. Has been superseded by better cultivars. Also used as a rootstock.

**Cate**

*M. tetraphylla.* Originated on the property of William R. Cate, Malibu, Calif. Nuts medium to large. Shell average thickness. Kernels 40% of nut, cream colored, crisp in texture, flavor good to very good. Ripens in late October and November continuing over a period of 6 to 8Ο²¬†weeks. Tree precocious, moderately hardy, shows no alternate bearing tendencies. The most widely adapted cultivar for commercial use in California.

**Dorado**

*M. integrifolia.* Originated in Hawaii. Introduced by Rancho Nuez Nursery. Medium-sized, uniform nuts, 7/8 to 1Ο²¬†inch in diameter. Kernel averages 35% of nut, oil content 75%. Tree medium-tall, upright, attractive. Begins to bear after 5Ο²¬†years, self-harvesting, cold resistant. Very productive, often yielding 65 or more pounds of nuts per year.

**Elimbah**

Originated in Australia. Imported into California by E. Westree. Thin shells. Kernel averages 45-50% of nut. Nuts tend to drop year-round.

**James**

*M. integrifolia.* Originated in La Habra Heights, Calif. Medium-sized, uniform nuts, about 1Ο²¬†inch in diameter. Kernel averages 40 to 42% of nut, quality high, flavor very good, oil content 75%. Tree very tall, columnar, precocious, often producing after 2 or 3Ο²¬†years. Self-harvesting. Yields more per acre than any other California cultivar, 60 or more pounds per tree when mature.

**Keaau**

*M. integrifolia.* Originated in Lawai Valley, Kalaheo, Kauai, Hawaii. Medium-sized nut, averaging about 80Ο²¬†nuts per pound; Shell smooth, medium brown, thin. Kernel 42-46% of nut, color light cream, quality good. Season August to November. Tree moderately vigorous, upright, very productive.

**Keauhou**

*M. integrifolia.* Originated in Kona, Hawaii by W.B. Storey. Medium to large nut, averaging about 54Ο²¬†nuts per pound. Shell very slightly pebbled, medium-thick. Kernel 37 to 40% of nut, quality tends to vary in different locations. Harvest season relatively short, with most of the crop maturing within about 3Ο²¬†months. Tree vigorous, yields well, extremely resistant to anthracnose.

**Vista**

Hybrid. Originated in Rancho Santa Fe, Calif. by Cliff Tanner. Small to medium-sized nut, 3/4 to 7/8Ο²¬†inch in diameter. Kernel averages 46% of weight of nut, flavor excellent, oil content 75%. Shell very thin, can be cracked in an ordinary hand cracker. Tree medium-sized, pyramidal, begins to bear after 3Ο²¬†years. Self-harvesting. Flowers pink. Recommended for both home garden and commercial plantings.

**Waimanalo**

*M. integrifolia.* Originated at the Hawaii Agricultural Experiment Station, Waimanalo, Hawaii. Large nuts, occasionally with twin halves. Shell relatively thick. Kernel 38-1/2% of nut, flavor good, oil content 75%. Tree medium-sized, pyramidal, productive, begins to bear after 5 years. Produces nuts in large clusters. Resistant to frost and disease. Grows well in cooler climates, particularly near the ocean. Also yields good crops inland.

## FURTHER READING

- Butterfield, Harry M. *A History of Subtropical Fruits and Nuts in California.*University of California, Agricultural Experiment Station. 1963.
- California Macadamia Society. *Macadamia Nut Trees for California Gardens.*Undated.
- California Macadamia Society. *Yearbook* 1955 to date.
- Facciola, Stephen. *Cornucopia: a Source Book of Edible Plants.* Kampong Publications, 1990. pp. 380-381.
- Hamilton, R. A. and E. T. Fukunaga. *Growing Macadamia Nuts in Hawaii.*University of Hawaii, Agricultural Experiment Station Bulletin 121. 1959.
- Ortho Books. *All About Citrus and Subtropical Fruits.* Chevron Chemical Co. 1985. pp. 59-61.
- Page, P. E., comp. *Tropical Tree Fruits for Australia.* Queensland Department of Primary Industries. 1984. pp. 150-160.
- Rosengarten, Frederick, Jr. *Book of Edible Nuts.* Walker and Co. 1984.
- Samson, J. A. *Tropical Fruits. 2nd ed.* Longman Scientific and Technical. 1986. pp. 282-284.

See [Index of CRFG Publications, 1969 - 1989](http://www.crfg.org/fg/xref/xref-m.html#macadamia) and annual indexes of [Fruit Gardener](http://www.crfg.org/fg/index.html) for additional articles on the macadamia.

* * *

[Here is the list of additional CRFG Fruit Facts.](http://www.crfg.org/pubs/frtfacts.html)

* * *

Questions or comments? [Contact us.](http://www.crfg.org/contact.html)

#### Fruit-Crops.com

<img src="../_resources/8f2303d6195cfabfc78929adf002d411.jpg" width="74" height="74"/>was developed as an online aid to the class 'Introduction to Fruit Crops' (HORT 3020) at UGA. The material is from the book that I wrote for HORT 3020 ('Introduction to Fruit Crops'), a book still used in the class today, and it is reliable as a reference for any internet-based or traditional college class.

Here you will find fruit horticulture and agriculture tips for an online hort degree program for distance learning but you don't need to be a horticulture major or even working on a bachelor's or Master's degree to use the site.

Over the years I have enjoyed hearing from students, teachers, professors, government officials, farmers, crop industry experts and others from all over the world about fruit crops. If you have a question or comment please do not hesitate to contact me.

Also, please feel free to cite this information without permission for non-commercial purposes.

Thanks for visiting,
Mark

About Mark:

On August 1, 2012, Mark Rieger took office as dean of the University of Delaware's College of Agriculture and Natural Resources.

Rieger served as associate dean and professor in the University of Florida's College of Agricultural and Life Sciences since 2006 and was interim dean in 2010-11. As associate dean, Rieger had major responsibilities in graduate programs, distance education, statewide degree completion programs, the honors program and international education.

Prior to joining the University of Florida faculty, he was a professor in the University of Georgia's Department of Horticulture from 1999-2006. He joined the University of Georgia faculty as an assistant professor in 1987 and was promoted to associate professor in 1993 and professor in 1999.

Rieger received a bachelor's degree in horticulture in 1982 from the Pennsylvania State University, a master's degree in horticulture in 1984 from the University of Georgia and a doctorate in horticultural sciences in 1987 from the University of Florida.

#

## Macadamia nut ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ *Macadamia integrifolia, M. tetraphylla*

### **MACADAMIA TAXONOMY**

Macadamia nuts belong to a relatively obscure family, the *Proteaceae*. Two species are cultivated for their nuts, although several others produce edible nuts:

1. *Macadamia integrifolia* Maiden and Betche.
2. *M. tetraphylla* L. Johnson.
**Cultivars**

Cultivars grown in Hawaii include ΟΔβ²§Υ€KakeaΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€KauΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€KeaauΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€MakaiΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€MaukaΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€PahalaΟΔβ²§β³Δ, and ΟΔβ²§Υ€PurvisΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Cultivars are distinguished by crown shape, and upright types, such as ΟΔβ²§Υ€KauΟΔβ²§β³Δ and ΟΔβ²§Υ€KeaauΟΔβ²§β³Δ, are being favored over more spreading types recently. ΟΔβ²§Υ€KeaauΟΔβ²§β³Δ produces medium nuts with high percent kernel (44 percent) and is high yielding. ΟΔβ²§Υ€CateΟΔβ²§β³Δ is one of the most widely grown M. tetraphylla cultivars in California.

### **ORIGIN OF *MACADAMIA INTEGRIFOLIA*, HISTORY OF CULTIVATION**

Both species are native to the east coast of Australia, from rain forest-like climates; *M. integrifolia* is more tropical in its requirements than *M. tetraphylla*. The Hawaiian industry and tropical production is based on the former, whereas the small production in southern California is based on the latter. Nuts were brought to Hawaii and California in the late 1800ΟΔβ²§¬≤s, but only the Hawaiians developed nut culture, whereas the Californians used it initially as an ornamental.

### **WORLD AND UNITED STATES MACADAMIA PRODUCTION**

World ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ (2004 USDA FAS) 92,923 MT or 204 million pounds. Worldwide acreage is likely to be well under 100,000 acres. The top macadamia-producing countries (percent of world production) follow:

1. Australia (36)
2. United States (23)
3. South Africa (16)
4. Guatemala (11)
5. Kenya (6)

United States ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ (2004 USDA FAS) 21,133 MT or 46 million pounds. (in-shell nuts). Virtually all production is in Hawaii on about 18,000 acres. The small volume of rough-shell macadamia produced in California is not included in USDA statistics. The value of the industry in 2004 was $33.1 million. Price paid to growers is about 65Ο²¬Δ/lb. Yields average about 3,000 pounds/acre. The United States consumes the majority of the worldΟΔβ²§β³Δs macadamia nuts, importing an amount almost equal to domestic production.

For the most up to date statistical data on United States and World production numbers please refer to the following two websites:

World: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Statistics Division (FAOSTAT).Ο²¬†[FAOSTAT](http://faostat3.fao.org/home/index.html)

United States: The United States Department of Agriculture National Agricultural Statistics Service (USDA Ag Stats).Ο²¬†[USDA Ag Stats](http://www.nass.usda.gov/Statistics_by_Subject/index.php)

### **MACADAMIA BOTANICAL DESCRIPTION**

**[<img src="../_resources/c60b13fd7f941321c7ea5aadb29ab117.jpg" width="183" height="266"/>](http://fruit-crops.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/12/macadamia-tree.jpg)Plant**: A medium sized, tropical evergreen tree, with spreading, full canopies, reaching widths of 30 ft and heights of 20-30 ft. Leaves are linear-obovate, 4-6ΟΔβ²§¬≥ long, sparsely dentate with sharp teeth, and thick; gives the overall impression of a large holly leaf.

**Flowers**: Flowers are perfect, and most cultivars are self-fruitful, but sometimes yield better when cross-pollinated. Flowering is not synchronized; trees may have flowers, immature and mature nuts all at one time. Flowers are borne on long, fragrant racemes (4-8ΟΔβ²§¬≥) of dozens of individual flowers, from lateral buds on 1-yr wood. Flowering occurs in mid-winter and nuts are harvested 7-8 months later in July-November, although some nuts mature more-or-less year round.

**Pollination**: Bees are the pollinators; cross-pollination is necessary for full production, but the degree of self-incompatibility varies.

**[<img src="../_resources/0f9a9f7348af315aa60d7e9267481546.jpg" width="141" height="270"/>](http://fruit-crops.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/12/macadamia-fruit.jpg)Fruit**: Botanically a drupe, the fleshy outer portion is removed to reveal the nut. Nuts number from 10-30 on crowded stalks, and are covered with thick, shiny green hulls (=mesocarp and exocarp), which are adherent to the shell (=endocarp). Nuts are round, with kernels enclosed in hard shells, with shelling percentages of 40%.[<img src="../_resources/17e56353db194b9e76ab9a2d5d21ef2f.jpg" width="216" height="258"/>](http://fruit-crops.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/12/macadamia-nut.jpg)

### **GENERAL CULTURE**

**Soils and Climate** ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Deep, well-drained, soils with a pH of 5.5 to 6.5 are best, but trees are grown on a wide variety of soils. In Hawaii, they grow well on lava rock soils. Although tropical trees, macadamias tolerate mild freezing (28-32 F), and do not tolerate excessive heat; In Hawaii, cool ocean breezes allow cultivation at sea level, but inland in the tropics, trees must be grown at 1500 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 3500 ft. They have no chilling requirement, but a seasonal change in temperature may help to synchronize bloom.

Propagation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Whip grafts or side wedge grafts are made on young seedling rootstocks .

**Rootstocks** ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Seedlings of rough-shelled (M. tetraphylla) cultivars are the best rootstocks because they are more vigorous and hardy than smooth-shelled seedlings.

Planting Design, Training, Pruning ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Tree spacing depends on growth habit of the cultivar and the use of filler trees. Final spacings of 30 to 35 feet apart are typical. Closely spaced hedgerow orchards are sometimes used in Australia, with trees planted at 10 ΟÉβÄî 20 feet and later thinned to prevent overcrowding.

Macadamias are trained to a central-leader framework over a period of several years, as they grow slowly.

### **MACADAMIA HARVEST, POSTHARVEST HANDLING**

Nuts are harvested largely by picking up by hand, although mechanical harvesters are being evaluated. Harvesting may extend over a 6-12 week period. Harvested nuts are de-hulled mechanically and dried to low water contents (< 2%). Nuts are cracked and sorted to remove off-color kernels and pieces of shell. Kernels are graded into 2 classes flotation; Grade I kernels (>72% oil) float and Grade II (<72% oil) sink.

### **THE MACADAMIAΟΔβ²§β³ΔS CONTRIBUTION TO DIET**

Most of the crop is used for confectionery, but whole kernels are roasted and salted and sold in jars/cans, usually in ΟΔβ²§≈™gourmetΟΔβ²§¬ù sections of markets.

Macadamias are considered to be among the finest table nuts in the world. They contain high quantities of oil, and are therefore very fattening.

the farmer was going to charge 200gh to manually weed and plough (mixing in compost and charcoal-biochar which he will make himself) 1 acre which would take him 1 week to do and then move onto the next acre.

--------------------------

As a teacher, I have worked with Children who are autistic. I will not sugar coat it. These children can test you. They see and feel things differently than other children. They can be overstimulated by too much input, too many sounds, too many changes to routine, too much visual stimuli. When they are on overload you will see the type of behavior you talked about. Some of the best ways I help the children I have worked with is teaching them ways to control and manage their behaviors when they are moving into overload. Teaching deep breathing helps as well as the words that will tell you he "needs a time aside" (not time out). He told you in the video he needed a break so he is halfway there to understanding how to control things. When he loses control it is best to stop what you are doing and simply say "you are not in control" leave where you are and find a quiet place where he can freely and quietly gain control. Often this time aside is a time when he can practice deep breathing, jump up and down or press on a wall or floor to release the tension. Once he is back in control you can help him understand what bothered him and how he can react in the future. Sometimes these children process social information slower than others so don't use too much language or expecting quick responses to directions. It just will not work. Instead, try using words that will give him time to react. In a while we will.... is a cue to let him now that something is changing. If he does not follow a direction you could say "we will decide/ talk about this again/ later. A firm "you are not in control" tells him he needs a time aside to regain control.

Above all, you are so right to try and spend more time with him. I have work with several children who are now doing well in school and have a network of friends that understand them. The children I have worked with have a gift of seeing the world in a way that is very special. We just need to slow down and respect how they learn and see the world. Good luck and God Bless. Your heart and love will see you and him through this.

title: Mayo.txt
updated: 2020-06-10 08:55:29Z
created: 2020-06-10 08:55:29Z
source: file://C:\Users\h\Desktop\KeepToText-master\KeepToText-master\Text\Mayo.txt
author: paapablankson@gmail.com
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

makes some of the best homemade mayonnaise! I like to use 3/4 cup hemp oil, and about 1/3 cup olive and 1/3 cup pumpkin seed oil (enough to equal 1 1/2 cups total of oil) per batch of mayonnaise. I place this with a raw egg in a narrow pot or jar, plus 2 tbs apple cider vinegar, 1/2 tsp salt, 1 tsp organic mustard, a little stevia, and a little cayenne pepper. Blend slowly with a stick blender. It's really good mayonnaise!

you don't have to pay any money, unlike all the other sites i found. on this site you give your mortgage details and they tell you if your mortgage is securitised, then you can choose to pay for a securitisation audit if you want.

http://www.whatliesinyourmortgage.com/
you just have to put in your email and then follow the instructions.

i jus sent them my details and i am reading anything i can find about securitisation.

title: Mr Oman Herbert Blankson
updated: 2025-05-26 15:18:04Z
created: 2013-07-13 19:49:17Z
source: http://eecomplaints.co.uk/ee-complaints-letter-templates/letter-5
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Mr Oman Herbert Blankson
14 Lanchester Way
New Cross
London
SE14 5HQ
The Disclosures Team,
Everything Everywhere,
Hatfield Business Park,
Hertfordshire,
AL10 9BW

25 July 2013
Dear Sir/Madam,

Reference No: 786113 Ο²¬†No.07817 234 646
***Re: A Subject Access Request for Debt (***
***Mr Oman Herbert BlanksonΟ²¬†14 Lanchester WayΟ²¬†New CrossLondonΟ²¬†SE14 5HQ)***

I am writing to make a subject access request under the Data Protection Act 1998 for any personal information you hold about me.

Please supply me with all data that you hold on me. This includes in particular, but is not limited to, the following:-

1. The original signed, executed credit agreement/s and any terms and conditions that applied to the accountΟ²¬†at the time the account was opened.

2. Where there has been any event in my account history over this period which has required manual intervention by any person, I require disclosure of any indication or notes which have either caused or resulted in that manual intervention, or other evidence of that manual intervention in relation to my account held with orange.

4. Documents relating to any insurance added to the account, including the insurance contract and terms and conditions, date it was added and deleted.

5. Details of any collection charges added to the account; specifically, the date it was levied, the amount of the charge, a detailed financial Breakdown of how the charges were calculated, and what the charge charges cover.

6. Specific details of the fees/charges levied by any other agency in respect of this account/these accounts and a detailed breakdown of said fees/charges and what each charge relates to and on what date said fees/charges were levied.

7. A genuine copy of any notice of fair use of my data as required by the Data Protection Act 1998

8. A list of third party agencies to whom you have disclosed my personal data and a summary of the nature of the information you have disclosed.

9. Copies of statements for the entire duration of the credit agreement.

** I enclose the statutory maximum fee of Ο²¬Θ10. You have 40 days in which to comply. If you have any queries or questions then please contact me on the above address.**

Yours Faithfully,
Mr H Blankson

against future job vacancies where appropriate.

customer service, business developer, liaising and marketing personnel.
I have good communication skills developed through
constant contact with different nationalities as well as presentation
skills from marketing products and services to clients. I am very
versatile and a quick learner as well as a high information retention
capacity.

I have a good working knowledge of the Microsoft office package and
internet for excellent research. Being a motivated individual who
enjoys working as part of a team. I am also target oriented and work
well under pressure.

I am looking for a part-time or full time administration role and can
work on a temporary of full time basis. I drive, am looking for a
salary Ο²¬†Ο²¬Θ18K - upwards.

Thank you and I look forward to hearing from you shortly.

title: New Note

created: 2014-12-01 06:15:10Z
completed?: no
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

2 Real Enterprises
PO Box X84
Jamestown
Accra
Ghana
T: +233(0)544 252 252
E: 2realenteerprises@gmail.com

2nd Deceember 2014

The Chief Executive
Food & Drugs Board
Accra

Dear Sir/Madam,

RE: APPLICATION FOR FOOD,DRUGS AND STANDARDS BOARD CERTIFICATION OF CHE MATE TEA

We hereby apply for Food and Drugs Authority registration and certifcation of Che Mate tea in the Republic of Ghana.

The Che Mate tea is produced and manufactured in Argentina and has been introduced by the manufacturers into the international markets since 2008 with one of its European sales offices located in the United Kingdom (UK).

Since its introduction into the UK market, Che Mate tea has recieved tremenous patronage and has become popular in households across the UK. Sales have also increased as a result of its unique characteristic properties as a healthy tea.

We have enclosed copies of the certficate of origin, phytosanitary certificate (Parts 1 & 2) and the certificate of inspection for the import of products from organic production into the European community that was undertaken by the manufacturers to detrmine the chemical compostition of (Yerba Mate 'Ilex paraguariensis') Che Mate tea. We have also added to this local certificates of analysis as specified by yourselves from the Ghana Standards Authority and the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research.

We thank you and look forward to hearng from you soon.
Yours Sincerely
Oman Herbert-Blankson
---

Naturopathic Nutrition
Duration: 2 years, 1 weekend per month,
plus 100 clinical hours per year during the 2-year course.

Year 1 - The Building Blocks of Nutrition

Water, protein, fats, carbohydrates, vitamins and minerals, amino acids, phytonutrients, antioxidants, superfoods (spirulina, algaes, wheat and barley grass, propolis, aloe vera, etc).

### Source: paapa_s notebook/NUT, CASHEW Anacardium occidentale The medium to l.md
Processed: 2026-03-13 12:05:13
---
title: NUT, CASHEW Anacardium occidentale The medium to l.md
updated: 2026-03-10T16:04:58.0000000Z
created: 2026-03-11 21:42:17Z
completed?: no
---

---
title: >-
  NUT, CASHEW Anacardium occidentale The medium to large, very spreading tree
  (to
updated: 2013-03-23 21:13:32Z
created: 2013-03-23 19:52:46Z
source: http://www.naturalhub.com/grow_fruit_and_nuts_in_tropical_areas.htm
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

**NUT, CASHEW Anacardium occidentale The medium to large, very spreading tree (to around 30 feet) carries a decorative, pear shaped, juicy and aromatic fruit which is ripe when the fruit start to fall from the tree. The fruit color varies, with some trees having yellow fruit, others red. There is quite a bit of difference in flavor from tree to tree, but are usually slightly astringent and quite refreshing, with the yellow form being distinctly sweeter. The ascorbic acid content has been measured at a quite high 146mg/100 grams of pulp, and others have reported higher values still. This compares with the oranges approximately 60mg/100gms pulp. The down sides are that the fruit are particularly attractive to fruit flies, and can also cause blistering to the mouth in some sensitive people. This may be caused by careless handling- the cashew nut that hangs as an appendage at the base of the fruit is encased within a shell that has a caustic oil within it which is released if the skin of the shell is damaged. This caustic oil or sap causes irritation to the skin, it's severity depending on individual sensitivity. Some people carefully twist off the nut and throw it away in favor of the fruit. The reason they don't bother cracking open the shell to get the nut is that the cashew has to be released from within the caustic containing shell by roasting at 350-500Ο²¬ΚF/180-200Ο²¬ΚC. This destroys the caustic oil. However, the oil can spurt as it is being heated, and damage eyes and skin, so the nuts have to be roasted in a covered container, or buried in fine sand to absorb the oil. Trouble is, the high temperature needed to cause the kernel to dry out also causes the evolution of a cloud of vaporised caustic oil! After roasting, the as yet unextracted nuts are washed in water and detergent to remove any residual caustic sap, then nuts cracked open, and the thin pellicle removed from around the kernel. No wonder cashew nuts are expensive.**

**
**

**NUT, MACADAMIA Ο²¬†Macadamia integrifolia, M.tetraphylla- Macadamia nuts are an excellent tree for the hominids food garden. The nuts are particularly nutritious. The commercial growers go for nuts with high oil content and low sugar content-low sugar so the nuts don't caramelise when they are toasted. The urban hominid should go for nuts with a high sugar content, then dry them rather than toast or roast them. Dried, they keep for about a year before there is any rancidity. Grafted trees are better than cutting grown trees, as cutting grown trees sometimes are blown over once they have become fairly tall. Macadamias can be pruned for convenience, and if left alone, some varieties can become very large and spreading. Cultivars derived from M.tetraphylla are the sweetest, and have the particular advantage of having a husk which splits well, releasing the nut. The leaves of tetraphylla cultivars have a slightly ''prickly' margin. Cultivars of M.integrifolia have lower sugar, smooth leaves, and tend not to release the nut from the husk, meaning they have to be hand picked. The long racemes of pale purplish pink or white flowers are wonderfully fragrant and abundant. Some cultivars have attractive reddish or bronze new growth.Ο²¬†**

**Any other than a poorly drained soil will do. Cross pollination is essential, or nut numbers will be in the ones or twos per raceme, instead of hanging in bunches. Macadamias are loved by rats, and immature fruit can be damaged by piercing and sucking bugs. Other than that they are pretty care free.**

**
**
Proteaceae

The macadamia nut is rich in protein, oil, iron, calcium, thiamine, riboflavin, and niacin. The subtropical date also has a high nutritive value. Rich in carbohydrates, it is a good source of vitamin A, potassium, and iron but is low in oils and sodium. The coconut is high in phosphorus, iron, proteins, and oilsΟΔβ²§βÄùin this case all saturated fatty acids, the consumption of which should be limited according to health recommendations. Coconut milk aids in balancing pH in the body due to its alkaline reaction.

Read more:Ο²¬†http://www.answers.com/topic/tropical-and-subtropical-fruit#ixzz2OOqHFw8f
---

Xz

spoke to adam ext 21307 who said he has had cus with same experience and he will apply bundles, however give him a few days as would like to calculate so i can pay Ο²¬Θ300 approx.Ο²¬† elisegood

when he came back said contact credit refferalls who should waive whole bill. result pay whole bill!

give something then take back!!!!!

credit agencies/ purchaseers

called ofcom 0207 981 3040 ref1208477275

amanda exec office 0800 079 0134

who are there, credit perchases no 1 has contacted me, ofcom, about. been trying to chase it but no 1 has come back september 11.

14 Lanchester Way

SE14 5HQ

3rd May 2012

Orange credit referral department
Everything Everywhere Ltd
Po box 350
Ashford
TW15 9DP

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing today about my a/c mentioned above. I had received a bill
from you after going on holiday for over Ο²¬Θ1000 for overseas internet
usage which was a complete shock to me as this is not my normal usage
pattern, however whilst communicating with Orange at the time nothing
was resolved. As this has been going on for some time I have been
disillusioned along with causing me great stress as the industry I
work within requires me to have a credit check every year and to be
honest I am a nervous wreck, moreover when I attempted to apply for a
another job I could not proceed due to the state of my credit file.

So last week I plucked up the courage to call you again, after I
watched a programme on the BBC where various people in a similar
situation had their debts written off. I had also realised that
Orange had introduced a cap on overseas internet usage, whilst my a/c
was in dispute.
I spoke to Adam (Extension: 21307) in your supervisory department last
week Friday who realised this and apologised but he said he can only
write off 90% of the amount. He then suggested I write to yourselves
to get 100% written off, my credit file amended and moreover so this
can be dealt with swiftly. He has so far has placed notes on my a/c.

I have attached my credit file for your reference and I hope you look
over this situation and respond as iΟΔβ²§β³Δve been long term customer and
had liked the service from orange up until this point.

Kind Regards

Oman Herbert-Blankson

Q- should be a Ο²¬Θ20 cap (inside or outside EU no protection), ive been exploted & now ive seen other cases being highlighted (Dawn Christie). I shouldnt have to pay any of this, complain to ofcom, moral duty, cant tell me what day downloads happened & which days. was chargged excessive amount, was not my normal usage pattern, sensible resolution.

Spoke to chris @ 13:02 on 27.4.12 then Adam (Supervisor) he will callback on wed

ext 21307

steve his mgr ...spk 2 gail 61443

A-

**Been a long time customer**
**Write to the creditagenciesconcerned re this a/c so i can clear my name**
**Want to keep my existing number**
**Want to stay if i can get my old or good deal**
**was thinking about going to another network**
**Caused stress**

**This has been going on for over a year & ive beendisillusioned& i had the courage to call again as i was my last chance to save my job, career & life.**

always pay my bills
every year 10% as loyal customer & minutes

3rd May 2012

Once I tried to obtain my credit report I found out that the goings on.
This has now effected me, in terms of job, lifestyle and so on.

will pay the default in full if the default can be taken off of my record, or pay Ο²¬Θ1 a week.

chris x india

address changed in December with out my knowledge,

just had the chip, chip had been used only found out when i realised i was not receiving bills/ checked my credit file.

Phone was in my safe.

want to report as affecting my work

actionfraud

po po ref: cad844818jan

want hime to contribute

i dint use it & its affecting my credit, i cant have on my file for six years.......therefore i have to pay, looking to pay the debt

how much are you looking to contribute

Jo, I did not create this bill & I've got every right to be upset but taking it out on him & being angry/ irrational is pointless as it will get personal. However, the bill is there 4 everyone 2 see so Kobby cannot try to wriggle out of not paying the bill or just paying around Ο²¬Θ300(as thats what Ive been hearing is deemed an acceptable amount), if it can't be reduced. I emphasize with him & told him ill call him b4 ill call orange(this happened b4 u 1st called me last night). ### Source: paapa_s notebook/orange candle pic.md
Processed: 2026-03-13 12:05:13
---
title: orange candle pic.md
updated: 2026-03-10T16:04:58.0000000Z
created: 2026-03-11 21:42:17Z
completed?: no
---

---
title: orange candle pic
updated: 2012-10-22 14:15:59Z
created: 2012-10-22 14:15:46Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Tools Needed:
Orange, Grapefruit, or Lemon
Knife
Any type of oil; Vegetable, Canola, Olive

Step 1:

Remove / eat the middle portion of the orange being careful to leave the middle stem like portion intact. I found removal easiest by using a sharp knife and digging out the middle starting with the edge closest to the outside of the orange.

Step 2:

After step 1, I usually let my orange sit out in the open inside for 2 hours just to dry out a little bit. Then I fill the cavity with any type of oil such as vegetable or olive oil to just below the center stem-like "wick". Light the center stem wick. My orange candle burned for most of the day. :)

~Sowing the Future
---

Plain and simple, soda provides nothing but empty calories, so itΟΔβ²§β³Δs best to strike it from your diet entirely. Thinking of just switching to diet or low-cal soda? Think again. Studies have shown than the artificial sweeteners in diet soda may cause cancer. So just skip the soda altogether and drink water:)

### Source: paapa_s notebook/ssm meeting.md
Processed: 2026-03-13 12:05:14
---
title: ssm meeting.md
updated: 2026-03-10T16:04:58.0000000Z
created: 2026-03-11 21:42:17Z
completed?: no
---

---
title: ssm meeting
updated: 2012-02-02 11:40:29Z
created: 2012-01-22 10:50:42Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Peter Ng introduction, Ο²¬†would like to branch into ghana via sport, may help with website, his company is ofsted accreditied, but not teir 4, he is looking to establish the company.

Thanks to peter for hosting the meeting, tarun for organising, notification of geores illness

proposal for tarun to be acting chairman

Apologies from Edna, Peter, Karen, Kumar

Tarun: Thanks to prof blsnkdonfor the placement & he prays for the speedy recovery of dr George Leung

Reading of minutes
Apoligies
additions
Acceptance:
Seconded: Oman

Matters arrising

- No feedback from graham webb
- Website devlopment, content, starting point

-enquire from bobby about phone numbers &content.
-Aqueel asked about about office

-spoke about CIM & Chartered institue of purchase...need to be unique & practical with the recognition of courses by CIM Aqueel sugessted terms of reference (TOR) so there is clarity on what SSM will surrender & what we may achieve.

We have experience, skills in our people and this is our advantage.....

we discussed the documents needed for QCA, possibility of using IUA offices, IUA is a franchise based business

Discussed if there would be any scope for the SSM linking up with IUA. Council for licenced conveyencers, target market local students.

Convocation with IPFM going ahead

Not going ahead with charitable status

Aqueel mentioned SSM needs to familiarise themselves with UKBA/QCA/NARIC/ASIC/BAC

Along with local government accreditation in Ghana (NAB)

Example business model: Awarding body in recognised in Pakistan. Courses being run in the UK then awarded in Pakistan

Mr Ng mentioned Professional qualifications Management Level 6 & 7 awarded by the University of Wales. Unwittingly awarding certificates by the way of bribes.

Printing of the journal. Aqueel stated MOU needs to be signed with Terms Of Reference which should state duration of agreement for 3 years then revert to original

Members- Once membership is established then the two bodies will swap, also promote financial and marketing methods.

The movement to journal unification was unanimously voted.

Lapel badges

Aquel mention it should be round as it is big in its current format. Is there a possibility for a re-design of the lapel badge with the word around the logo.

NCFE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ need to have our documents read

Movement to thesis based assessment

Take note of space given to SSM in journal.

SNNP

Minutes read by Tarun
Same as SSM

Mrs Williams is the chairwoman but we need a chief executive.
Edna is unwell

We will contact the early years centre for further information, advertisement and possible recommendations.
---

### Source: paapa_s notebook/ssm.md
Processed: 2026-03-13 12:05:14
---
title: ssm.md
updated: 2026-03-10T16:04:58.0000000Z
created: 2026-03-11 21:42:17Z
completed?: no
---

---
title: ssm
updated: 2012-06-10 13:19:35Z
created: 2012-06-09 16:18:41Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Welcome
Present Absent
Introduction
Reading of minutes from the 58th Council Meeting by Tarun
Seconded by Tarun Matters Arising

No hear from Greaeme Webb re. QCA but meanwhile he advised that business/sales/marketing awarding bodies have unified their programmes for clarity

-greaeme web is directly involved with a college so has hands on approach.

MINUTES OF THE 59 COUNCIL MEETING HELD ON 21 MAY 2012AT THE 14 LANCHESTER WAY, NEW CROSS, SE14 5HQ

1  **WELCOME**  : Before the chairman, Professor George Leung, opened the meeting, at 2.15 pm, Dr TarunΟ²¬† Pathak apologized for being late by 15 minutes. Apology accepted.

**2. APOLOGIESΟ²¬† :Ο²¬†Ο²¬†**  Apologies were received from
Ms. Karen Scarles
Mr. Bobby Rajah
Lady Edna Crabbere
Sir Peter Crabbere

PRESENT:
Dr Tarun Pathak
Prof. Sir Robert Herbert-Blankson
Kingsley Abrams
Belinda Washington
Mr. Oman Herbert-Blankson

3. The chairman read the minutes of the 58  th  Council meeting held on 21 August 2010

Ο²¬†4. MATTERS ARISING: (a)Ο²¬†Ο²¬† No contact from Graeme Webb re. QCA/Ofqual appilcation feedback but meanwhile he advised that business/sales/marketing awarding bodies have unified their programmes for clarity. Using the Qualifcation Credit framework.

-Graeme Web is directly involved with a college so has hands on approach.

(b) Website content need updating as it is old and needs a re-fresh. With access available to some council members.

(c) Thesis based study instead of exam based study to reduce costs was discussed. Also may have to explore a non Ofqual accreditation route.

(d) Funding/ Premises. Need a dedicated office & team so we can start building the business. Two weeks was given to find office space. Need to source Ο²¬Θ5000 seed capital to gain charitable status then we can apply for funding.
---

title: Stocks intro
updated: 2025-05-26 18:00:39Z
created: 2012-03-01 21:30:19Z
completed?: no
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

Thanks to Vic & Chuck for letting me in!! (i try to come out but they just pull me back in) Quote of the day!

Hi, to everyone in LVS!

My original plan was to stay in long on stocks, buyingΟ²¬† in a Ο²¬Θ1000 bundles an placing them in an isa. But after all the ups

and downs in TLW which i've been following & have holding for a year now, I thought that I could make some money with a quicker return if I actually knew what I was doing.

Have holdings in AFR, TLW, CZA & NTOHAA (New Star's Heart of Africa Fund) which has been wound up due reduced liquidity in Sub-Saharan markets!

So after placing a portfolio on google to watch the market, read & research new stocks I met VicVan on the AFR board & here I am.Ο²¬† I have an interest in the oil in Ghana as that is my origin so been researching & co-incidentally investing in the exploratory companies TLW & AFR. Albeit a newbie am always learning and believe it or not people do ask me for investment advice as I have done well in the property market.Ο²¬† My areas of stock market interest are Gold, oil, minerals & green technology.Ο²¬† But will diversify into good plays.... looking at casinos at the moment like (LVS) & just cottoned on that this forum is named after this stock. What a coincidence, feels like i was always supposed to be here!!

Live in East Dulwich, London, UK & have done all my life. Have Degree in Business & Marketing, starting Masters in Geo-tagging.

Area of expertise Mobile phone & laptop sales & repair.

Regards & good luck to all!

Paapa

Hi,
I would like to join your forum via invite from Steve aka VicVan.
I have an interest in the oil in Ghana as that is my origin & co-
incidentally investing in the exploratory companies TLW & AFR, albeit
a newbie.
So my areas of investment interest are Gold, oil, other natural
resources & green technology. But will diversify into good plays....
looking at casinos at the moment (LVS).

Have Degree in Business & Marketing, starting Masters in Geo-tagging.

Area of expertise Mobile phone & computer sales & repair.

Regards
Paapa

---
title: swiss post
updated: 2025-05-26 15:18:05Z
created: 2013-07-11 15:49:10Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

I am writing this because of the problems I have had when using my swiss post box. When I set up the account I registered my credit card only to find after a few months to have not been registered and no payments were taken I received an e-mail from your customer service account apartment to update my card details which I did. After which to my amazement I found out my account had closed. I have paid over in above the amount which is due so I'm very surprised my account has been closed also the responses I have had from your customer services department have been less than accommodating as I am a genuine customer which wishes to patronise your services after they were highly recommended. I have decided to send a message as I am highly disappointed and hope to get this matter resolved as soon as possible. I hope to hear from you soon.

Thank you

Herbert
---

---
title: TARUN MURTI PATHAK
updated: 2012-08-31 15:48:44Z
created: 2012-08-31 15:48:44Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

TARUN MURTI PATHAK
101, Otley Drive, Gants Hill, Ilford, IG2 6QY

Tel/Fax: +44 20 8554 8856; E-mail: +44 7966 216835; E-mail: tpathak007@aol.com; Nationality: British

PERSONAL PROFILE

I have a dynamic career in the Business & IT educational sector working as a freelance lecturer and an educational consultant. I am self-motivated and energetic and possess excellent interpersonal skills, along with the ability to communicate effectively at all levels to people from varying backgrounds. I have travelled extensively around the world to teach, thus making me flexible and adaptable to the diverse markets. I am able to bring the quality of my experience and creativity to new ideas and challenges, and am able to work under immense pressure.

CURRENT POSITION
2011 to date London Premier College

Senior Lecturer in Strategic Human Resources Management and Project Management for the Organisation for Tourism & Hospitality Management Higher Diploma Level 6.

2010 to date SDS College

Senior Lecturer in Corporate Strategy and Managing Finance for the NCC PgD in Business Management. Ο²¬†Have also taught Marketing Management, HRM and Management Behaviour for the Association for Tourism & Hospitality Executives Postgraduate Diploma.

2008 to date London International College

Senior Lecturer in Marketing Management, Organisational Behaviour, Research Project and Corporate Strategy. I have taught on the University of Wales and University of Sunderland Bachelors and Masters programmes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ all validated by RDI.

I have also taught on the University of Northern Virginia (USA) Undergraduate and Postgraduate programme.

2008 to date Maritime Greenwich College

Senior Lecturer. Have taught on the Association of Business Executives Diplomas, Advanced Diplomas and Postgraduate Diplomas in Organisational Behaviour, Strategic HRM, Marketing and Corporate Strategy.

Have also taught on the Gibaran Learning Group Bachelors and Masters of Business Administration programmes in Operations Management, Marketing Management, Strategic Management and Strategic HRM.

EDUCATION

2009 to date Diploma in Teaching for the Lifelong Learning Sector (DTLLS) Ο²¬†from East London College

1999 to date PhD in MARKETING

1997 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 1998 MASTERS OF BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION IN MARKETING

1996 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 1997 POSTGRADUATE DIPLOMA IN BUSINESS

1993 - 1996 BA (Hons) in BUSINESS ACCOUNTING

1991 - 1993 AΟΔβ²§β³Δ Levels in Accounting and Chemistry (1993), an a GCSE (General Certificate in Secondary Education) in Computer Studies (1992).

1990 - 1991 GCSE (General Certificate in Secondary Education) in French, Chemistry, English, German, Mathematics and Accountancy.

EXAMINERSHIPS

2010 to date SDS College

First marker for the NCC PgD papers of Management of Financial Information & Resources and Strategic Management. Have also been first marker for the ATHE PgD papers of HRM, Management Behaviour and Marketing Management.

2008 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2009 Grafton College & Maritime Greenwich College

Have marked course work assignments for the Gibaran MBA in Strategic HRM, Strategic Operations Management and Strategic Financial Issues. Have also marked course work for the Gibaran BBA Operations Management.

2008 to date Kensington College of Business

Am an examiner for the International Business Diploma and the MBA Qualifying programme in subjects including Marketing, Human Resources Management, Financial Accounting and Management Accounting.

Have assisted in marking dissertations for the University of Wales MBA in December 2009.

2008 to date London International College

Have set and marked examination papers for the collegeΟΔβ²§β³Δs short courses and also for the University of Northern Virginia Bachelors and Masters programmes.

2004 to date International University of America

Have set and marked examination papers in a variety of subjects for the Bachelors and Masters programmes.

2003 to date Association of Chartered Certified Accountants

A checker to Examination Paper 3.5 Strategic Planning & Development. This involved checking the papers that have been marked, along with the CRSs to see that the marks tally. Have provided assistance in preparing the ExaminerΟΔβ²§β³Δs report.

2002 to 2004 London College of Advanced Studies

Have set and marked examination papers for St. ClementsΟΔβ²§β³Δ University Bachelors and Masters programmes.

1999 to 2002 London Institute of Technology & Research

Have set and marked examination papers for a variety of American universityΟΔβ²§β³Δs Bachelors and Masters programmes. They include the New York Institute of Technology, University of Stamford USA and the International University, Missouri, USA

PROFESSIONAL MEMBERSHIPS

2009 to date Organisation for Tourism & Hospitality Management

2005 to date Fellow Members of the Society of Sales & Marketing & the Society of Nursery Nursing Practitioners

WORK EXPERIENCE

EDUCATIONAL CONSULTANCY

Self-employed educational consultant of Global-Study.Net which recruits international students to a wide range of overseas institutions. Global-Study.Net has a vast network of agents in various countries notably the Middle East, the Indian Subcontinent, the Far East, Africa and, now, South America.

Knowledge of the international legal framework of employment legislation and regulatory environment in relation to day to day operations of Global-Study.Net. Experience of dealing with people with a variety of social and ethical backgrounds in relation to the business development of Global-Study.Net.

Consulting Business Developer for the University Partnership Consultants ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a consultancy that designs course programmes for UK university moderation, validation and articulation, as well as looking into UK university affiliations and partnerships.

Prepared course validation documents for a number of commonwealth universities, notably Charles Sturt (Australia), Northumbria (UK), East London (UK), Wales (UK), etc. in order to help colleges gain university affiliations and articulations.

Consultant to a number of private colleges in setting up a number of professional courses, e.g. the Chartered Institute of Management Accountants, the Chartered Association of Certified Accountants; and academic university programmes, for example courses provided by NCC Education and Edexcel Diploma in Management Studies.

Acted in an advisory capacity to a number of private colleges who successfully applied for accreditation by the following UK bodies: British Accreditation Council, ASIC & DfES.

I have further made Guildhall College a UK franchise for Informatics Holdings, Singapore to run their distance-learning programmes, offered via Purple Train.

LECTURING

Have been a freelance lecturer at various UK Colleges teaching Business Management, Marketing Management, Basic Accounting and similar subjects at various undergraduate, postgraduate and professional levels.

EMPLOYMENT

Employed as a business manager in the Rush Green Medical Centre. Responsibilities include: strategic planning, financial planning and developing a financial tracking system, day to day operations management, and fund holding management.

REFERENCES
Available Upon request
---

---------------
Explanation
-Corrupt world leaders are animals with no country (greedy, ruthless)
-the outside world is crazier and more dangerous than prison

**Muhammadu Buhari took over as Head of State via a 1983 coup d'etat (deposing Shagari). He & his second in command Tunde Idiagbon persecuted Fela. In 1984, they had Fela arrested on fake foreign currency charges prior to an international tour to prevent him from "publishing anything that might embarrass any government official"- even if true (which they had formally made illegal). Fela's trial judge Okoro Idogu admitted that he was ordered by Idiagbon to find Fela guilty & give him a harsh sentence- the judge later begged to dismiss Fela's conviction. Fela served 5 years in prison.

Buhari & Idiagbon also instituted a campaign against "indiscipline" known as "War Against Indiscipline" (WAI).

-Pieter Willem Botha was the militantly racist white prime minister of apartheid era South Africa. A cartoonish super-villain, he made almost unbelievably racist public statements, laws, and atrocious violations of human rights (including torture & murder) against blacks in South Africa.

Coffin for Head of State- Explanation

A criticism of arbitrary/artificial religious & ethnic boundaries, and of religious hypocrisy- people who invoke god, but then commit atrocities, including Obasanjo (General) & YΟΔβ²§β³Δaradua (Leiutentent Colonel), two of FelaΟΔβ²§β³Δs attackers.

The poor live in squalor while the pastor lives in luxury. Fela walks (waka), & sees the unhappiness of his people,and the corruption & greed of the colonial-influenced Nigerian government. Colonial culture confuses the African people.

In 1978, when FelaΟΔβ²§β³Δs mother (a government official & activist) dies from injuries inflicted by the Army during an attack on FelaΟΔβ²§β³Δs compound, Fela and his Movement of the People Carry an effigy of his motherΟΔβ²§β³Δs coffin to the barracks of two noted Fela enemies, Gen. Obasanjo & Lt.Crnl. YΟΔβ²§β³Δaradua. As a protest, Fela & MOP put the coffin down, and force the army to take it (Obasanjo & Yaradua eventually help to carry it). ΟΔβ²§≈™It remains there. ΟΔβ²§¬ù

***********************************************************************
COLOMENTALITY

Explanation

Derides authorities who locked Fela in Alagbon prison on drug charges, after eating the evidence.

The police monitored him closely, collecting his "expensive shit," only to find it clean as a baby's (Carlos Moore, "Fela Fela This Bitch of a Life").

INTERNATIONAL THIEF THIEF (explanation)

* Uses abbreviation of ΟΔβ²§≈™International Telephone & TelegraphΟΔβ²§¬ù to attack all foreign companies for stealing from Africa, and for turning Africans into human-waste carriers [agbepo]. Derides stupid Africans who are put into power as puppets of the companies. Tricked & used by these companies, they become equally greedy & ruthless.

ΟΔβ²§≈™I still want to speak the truth. If I lie, may I be punished by Edumare (a Yoruba god), Osiris, the Ifa (Nigeria gods), & the land itself. I have read about it read it in books, I have seen it personally.

Europeans came & forced their ways on Africa. Long ago, Africans didn't carry shit, we shat in a big hole (as evidenced by words for this hole in many African tribal languages-Yoruba, Igbo, Haussa, Ashanti ΟΔβ²§¬Π). When the Europeans came to colonialise us, it was part of their culture to carry shit & taught us Africans to do it, too. They have turned us into agbepo, waste carriers (the lowest members of society).

Foreign companies confuse & trick Africans by manipulating (English) words in the newpaper. They subvert & control Africa using the same method: they find a stupid, greedy, & dishonest African, pay him a million Naira (Nigerian money), he bribes others to become a chief, but who acts in European interests. Like a rat, creeps here and there, gaining power by befriending journalists, religious leaders, & government officials.

Once in power, he begins embezzling, inflation, confusion, oppression...

Two examples of this are Obasanjo (Head of the military) and Abiola (CEO of International Telephone & Telegraph) . We must fight them. We refuse to carry their shit any longer.

Kalakuta show-o-o

Kalakuta show o-o
Kalakuta show
*EtcΟΔβ²§¬Π.

>Organ solo
>Sax solo

>End with bass/percussion under solo sax

-------------------
Explaination:
Kalakuta was Fela's compound (houses around a courtyard).

This song describes one of many violent police attacks on Kalakuta, this time for "abducting" a girl who sought shelter there. She, of course testified in support of Fela- who was eventually exonerated.

Fela stated the attack was "so fucking terrible that I don't want to talk about it anymore" (Carlos Moore, "Fela Fela This Bitch of a Life" p.129)

Explanation

African women are embarrassed to be called "African", but African women should not aspire to Western sensibilities (i.e. be a proper Western "Lady").

MR. GRAMMARTICALOGYLISATIONALISM : Explanation

A criticism of African Educational system as a ΟΔβ²§Υ€poor imitationΟΔβ²§β³Δ of the Western Education system. The man who speaks better English gets paid more. The schoolΟΔβ²§β³Δs grades are rated (better bread as climb). The English use words against us-- first thing in the morning we are given newspapers, they brainwash us with big words. ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs all jargon/nonsense-- irrelevant issues that have no bearing on common men. Then the leaders blame the poorΟΔβ²§β³Δs problems on ignorance & delinquency, but itΟΔβ²§β³Δs the (English speaking) leaders who are delinquent.

Question Jam Answer: Explanation

People are selfish. Mr. Question stops (jams) Mr. Answer on the road, a surprising thing will happen.

For example:
As you sit, someone steps on your leg,
You inquire- ΟΔβ²§≈™why did you step on me, didnΟΔβ²§β³Δt you see my leg?ΟΔβ²§¬ù
You expect an apology & sympathy

Instead, he responds, ΟΔβ²§≈™Why did you put your leg in my way,
DidnΟΔβ²§β³Δt you see me coming?

--------------
Explanation

Details the 1977 police attack on ("the dissident") Fela's Kalakuta compound (14-A Agege Motor Rd)- precipitated by Fela's nursing of a street teen beaten some policemen for mouthing off. Police cut Fela's fence, burned his cars & house, beat his men, beat and raped his wives, threw his 77 year-old mother out of a window (she eventually died from her injuries). Fela was imprisoned for almost a month. A government investigation found Fela's compound was wrongly attacked by "unknown soldiers."

_____________________________________

WATER NO GET ENEMY: EXPLANATION

The "water" is the Nigerian common people. The Government/Military must not make enemies of the populous, nor disturb their "flow" (endevours & routines)- as it is dangerous to make an enemy of the country's most essential resource. The people make everything happen.

Nothing exists without water
Water does not gain enemies
because if you fight (renounce)water, you will die

Power cannot exist without the peopleΟΔβ²§¬Π

title: The Ghanaian marriage ceremony
updated: 2012-08-17 15:51:40Z
created: 2012-08-17 12:40:16Z
---

<div style="display: none;">ΟΔβ²§≈†</div>

The Ghanaian marriage ceremony

The Ghanaian marriage ceremony is a traditional ceremony where the groom accompanied by his family formally asks for the bride's hand in marriage in the presence of family, friends and well wishers. The traditional ceremony is a necessary common rite of marriage for all Ghanaian couples. In Ghana today, some couples perform this alone as a marriage ceremony, however, most couples also go on to perform the western wedding in a church in addition to the traditional marriage ceremony.

Ο²¬†The marriage ceremony starts with the "knocking" (kokooko) on the door ceremony. In the knocking ceremony the groom, along with his father and some elder members of the family visits the brides house to announce their marriage intentions. Often times this ceremony is performed a week or two before the actual marriage ceremony. The knocking ("kookoo ko") is derived from the Ghanaian tradition of knocking on at the entrance of a house before entering as a visitor. For the knocking ceremony the groom's family brings along two bottles on Schnapps (alcoholic drinks), some money and cola to the house to present to the brides family. In the past, and to date, the drinks are used to pour libation. (Libation is a traditional form of prayer to the ancestral spirits and God). Ο²¬†When the drinks are presented, a designated spokesman from the grooms delegation formally asks the brides family for permission to enter the house and announce their intentions. If the drinks are accepted then it means permission has been granted to the visitors to state their intentions. The spokes person will then explain in the most lyrical language, that the groom, has seen a "beautiful flower" in the house of the brides family that he desires and would like to "uproot" that flower, not steal, from its keeper, hence they are here to ask for the brides hand in marriage and inquire about what is required in order to make that flower his own.

Once the intentions are announced, the brides family may ask the groom and his family to come back at a set later date during which the brides family will investigate the grooms family background further to see

a) if the family has no chronic illness or genetic disabilities in the family

b) if family has a good reputation, that is no immediate family member such as a sibling, an aunt or uncle is known to be a thief, prostitute or murderer

c) if the groom has no illegitimate children or has another marriage elsewhere etc.

d) if the groom is of good character and well matched to the bride

Often times the background inquiry is made when the brides family knows nothing or knows little of the groom's family. If they are satisfied and pleased with what they find out, they will send a list of things to the groom and his family to provide before they can marry the bride. This list is commonly made up of, but not limited to,the following, :

a) dowry /or bride price- often an undisclosed sum of money, 2 Ο²¬†bottled drink of whisky or Gin

b) Money for the brides father, 1 bottle of expensive whisky & a piece of cloth

c) A pair of sandals and money for the bride of the mother

d) Jewelry for the bride

e) At least 6 pieces of traditional wax print cloth for the bride

f) Engagement ring

g) Engagement bible

h) Shoes, headpieces, other gifts for the bride

j) Drinks, money and food for the guests that will be present to witness the ceremony

k) Money for the brothers or male cousins (if bride has no brothers)

On the set date the groom and his family, along with invited guests show up early at the brides house. The grooms family sits on one side, while the other brides family sits on the other side facing each other. Elders from both family begin the marriage ceremony with a prayer and introductions. The grooms family begins by presenting the dowry and all the other items on the list one, by one. At each stage, the items are checked to make sure everything asked on the list is being presented. Negotiation is possible if the grooms family feels too much is being asked of them. The bride is not present in all of these proceedings. The groom, although present, does speak in all of these proceedings as all the speaking and negotiation is done on his behalf by the designated spokes person from his family.

Once everything has presented to the brides family, the bride would then be brought into the gathering. Because a decoy can be used to "tease" the groom, the groom is asked to verify if this is indeed his bride. Once he confirms, she is asked three times by her father if she agrees to marrying the groom. She is asked if they should accept the dowry and accompanying gifts from the grooms family. When she agrees, then the groom will slide the ring onto her fingers and kiss and hug her. An elder presents a bible to both the groom and bride as a symbol of how important religion should be in their married life. Prayers are said and blessings are given. The married couple is now congratulated and each elder in the room offers marriage advice to the new couple. Once all of this is done there is a huge celebration/reception where food & drinks are served. There is lots of music and dancing till nightfall.
---
